scenario_title: Delete absent RR after present prerequisite in same UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates the gap between prerequisite truth and exact-RR deletion semantics. The zone starts with h.campus.edu. A 192.0.2.70, so rrset_exists on h.campus.edu. A is satisfied. The subsequent delete_rr targets h.campus.edu. A 192.0.2.71, which is not a member of the RRset. RFC 2136 does not clearly require a particular RCODE or mandate whether the server must treat the operation as a no-op versus a semantic error, so bind and knot can legitimately diverge on the UPDATE reply while leaving the zone unchanged or handling the delete benignly.

group_1.behavior: Treat the absent exact RR delete as a benign no-op after the prerequisite succeeds.

group_2.behavior: Reject the UPDATE as a semantic failure when the specific RR to delete is not present.
group_2.post_state: h.campus.edu. remains unchanged with A 192.0.2.70 only.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.1 and §3.4.2 define prerequisite handling and UPDATE processing, but §3.4.2.2 does not precisely specify the required outcome when delete_rr names an RR that is absent from an RRset that already satisfied a prerequisite. As a result, implementations may either ignore the absent RR and succeed or fail the UPDATE with NXRRSET/SERVFAIL, making both reply code and processor behavior under-specified.
